post.description: Creates a new account from Firebase identity data and returns a token pair. Enforces username uniqueness, email uniqueness, and username format rules. Status is set to 'pending' until email is verified by Firebase.
Authenticates the user via Firebase UID and issues a token pair. Rate-limited to 5 attempts / 15 min per UID. Banned, blocked, or deleted accounts are rejected with 403.
Issues a new token pair from a valid refresh token. The old refresh token is revoked (rotation).
Revokes both tokens. The user is logged out of this device only.

get.description: Returns the full private profile of the authenticated user.

put.description: Updates allowed fields for the authenticated user. Only `username` and `profilePicture` can be changed here. Email changes require a separate verification flow. Status can never be changed via this endpoint.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the authenticated user's own account (sets status = deleted). Only a user can delete their own account (§1.4).

get.description: Returns a user's public profile. Blocked users cannot view the profile of their blocker (§3.3).
Returns all users (admin use).

put.description: Updates the status of an existing user. Admin action — creates audit log type=5.

get.description: Returns all chats where the authenticated user is a participant.

post.description: Opens a chat with another user (§4.1). Users must be accepted friends (§3.4). If an active chat already exists between the two users, it is returned instead of creating a duplicate. New chats start with status = pending.

get.description: Returns a specific chat. Only participants may access it (§4.3).

post.description: Activates a pending chat (pending → enabled). Either participant may accept. Once enabled, messages can be sent (§4.1, §5.1).
Closes the chat (§4.2). Either participant may end it. Sets endedAt = now and status = disabled. No new messages can be sent afterwards.
REjects a pending chat (pending → deleted). Either participant may reject. Once deleted, messages cannot be sent.

get.description: Returns all messages for a chat. Only participants may access them (§5.2, RLS).

schema.title: Response Getmessagesbychatid Messages Chat  Chatid  Get

get.summary: Get unread messages in a chat
get.description: Returns all unread messages in the given chat.

schema.title: Response Getunreadmessagesbychatid Messages Chat  Chatid  Unread Get

get.description: Returns a specific message.

post.description: Sends a message to a chat (§5.1). The chat must be active (enabled or pending — pending chats auto-activate on first message). Content is sanitised; empty content after sanitisation is rejected. status = unread, sendAt = now.

put.description: Marks a specific message as read (§5.3). Idempotent — already-read messages are not updated.
Marks all unread messages in a chat as read (§5.3).

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's active streak. Auto-expires and resets the streak if no activity was recorded in the last 24 h (§6.3).
Returns the authenticated user's longest streak (isRecord = True).
Returns all past and current streaks for the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new active streak. Fails with 409 if an active streak already exists (§6.1). start = now, isRecord = False.
Manually ends the active streak, checks whether it is a new personal record, and immediately starts a fresh streak (§6.2). Creates an audit log entry of type 7.
Confirms the user's sobriety for today, refreshing the streak's activity timestamp. Must be called at least once every 24 h to prevent auto-expiry (§6.3).

get.description: Returns all badges.

post.description: Creates a new badge.

get.description: Returns a specific badge by its ID.

post.description: Updates the status of an existing badge.

get.description: Returns all user badges by userId.
Returns all user badges by badgeId.

schema.title: Response Getbybadgeid User Badges  Userbadgeid  Get

delete.description: Soft deletes an existing user badge.

post.description: Grants a badge to a user.
Revokes a badge from a user.
Updates the status of an existing user badge.

get.summary: Get all audit logs for current user
get.description: Returns all audit logs for the authenticated user. RLS policies ensure users only see their own logs.

post.description: Creates a new audit log entry.

get.description: Returns a specific audit log by its ID.
Returns all audit logs filtered by type (e.g., 1=login, 2=password_change).

schema.title: Response Getauditlogsbytype Logs Type  Logtype  Get

get.description: Returns all audit logs for a specific catalyst (user).

schema.title: Response Getauditlogsbycatalyst Logs Catalyst  Catalystid  Get

get.summary: Get audit logs by date range
get.description: Returns all audit logs within a specified date range.

schema.title: Response Getauditlogsbydaterange Logs Range By Date Get

get.description: Returns all friendships for the authenticated user.
Returns all pending friendship requests received by the authenticated user.
Returns all pending friendship requests sent by the authenticated user.
Returns a specific friendship. Only participants may access it.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the friendship. Only participants may remove it.

get.description: Returns the blocked user of a friendship.

post.description: Sends a friend request to the specified user (§3.1). Cannot send to yourself, cannot send if a non-deleted friendship already exists, and cannot send to a user who has blocked you.
Accepts a pending friend request (§3.2). Only the recipient of the request can accept it.
Rejects (ignores) a pending friend request (§3.2). Only the recipient of the request can reject it.
Blocks a user within a friendship (§3.3). Either participant may block the other at any time, regardless of current status.
Unblocks a previously blocked user.

type.title: Type
type.description: Audit log type (ex: 1 login, 2 password change, etc.)

type.title: Type
type.description: Audit log type (ex: 1 login, 2 password change, etc.)

UserResponse.title: UserResponse
UserResponse.description:
Schema for the client response.
Has the fields created by the database (ID and timestamps).
